samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Implement an 8-bit unsigned multiplier using pipelined processing. Use sequential logic where necessary.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal for chip reset.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal controlling the multiplication process.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplicand input signal with a width of 8 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplier input signal with a width of 8 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating the completion of multiplication.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output signal with a width of 16 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">State IDLE = 0.</state>
        <state id="LOAD">State LOAD = 1.</state>
        <state id="CALC">State CALC = 2.</state>
        <state id="OUT">State OUT = 3.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_en_out_reg" width_description="[3:0]">Register to hold the output enable signal during multiplication.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_en_out_next" width_description="[3:0]">Next state for output enable signal.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplicand.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_a_next" width_description="[7:0]">Next state for multiplicand register.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplier.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_b_next" width_description="[7:0]">Next state for multiplier register.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the first partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the second partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the third partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the fourth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the fifth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the sixth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[6]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the seventh partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="temp[7]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the eighth partial product.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the first partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the second partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the third partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fourth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_out_reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the final product.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_out_next" width_description="[15:0]">Next state for product register.</reg>
        
        // Sequential logic for output enable signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                mul_en_out_reg <= 4'd0;
            end else begin
                mul_en_out_reg <= mul_en_out_next;
            end
        end
        
        // Combinational logic for next state of output enable signal
        always @(*) begin
            case(mul_en_out_reg)
                4'd0: mul_en_out_next = {3'd0, mul_en_in};
                4'd1: mul_en_out_next = 4'd2;
                4'd2: mul_en_out_next = 4'd3;
                default: mul_en_out_next = 4'd0;
            endcase
        end
        
        // Sequential logic for multiplicand register
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                mul_a_reg <= 8'd0;
            end else begin
                mul_a_reg <= mul_a_next;
            end
        end
        
        // Combinational logic for next state of multiplicand register
        always @(*) begin
            case(mul_en_out_reg)
                4'd0: mul_a_next = mul_a;
                default: mul_a_next = mul_a_reg;
            endcase
        end
        
        // Sequential logic for multiplier register
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                mul_b_reg <= 8'd0;
            end else begin
                mul_b_reg <= mul_b_next;
            end
        end
        
        // Combinational logic for next state of multiplier register
        always @(*) begin
            case(mul_en_out_reg)
                4'd0: mul_b_next = mul_b;
                default: mul_b_next = mul_b_reg;
            endcase
        end
        
        // Combinational logic for generating partial products
        genvar i;
        generate
            for(i = 0; i < 8; i = i + 1) begin : pp_gen
                assign temp[i][15:0] = ({8{mul_b_reg[i]}} & mul_a_reg);
            end
        endgenerate
        
        // Sequential logic for storing partial sums
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                sum[0] <= 16'd0;
                sum[1] <= 16'd0;
                sum[2] <= 16'd0;
                sum[3] <= 16'd0;
            end else begin
                sum[0] <= temp[0];
                sum[1] <= temp[1] + temp[2];
                sum[2] <= temp[3] + temp[4];
                sum[3] <= temp[5] + temp[6] + temp[7];
            end
        end
        
        // Sequential logic for calculating final product
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                mul_out_reg <= 16'd0;
            end else begin
                mul_out_reg <= sum[0] + sum[1] + sum[2] + sum[3];
            end
        end
        
        // Assigning outputs based on output enable signal
        assign mul_en_out = mul_en_out_reg[3];
        assign mul_out = mul_en_out ? mul_out_reg : 16'd0;
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>
        On the positive edge of the clock signal, sample the input enable signal and store it in the register, mul_en_out_reg.
        Output enable signal, mul_en_out, is derived from the most significant bit of mul_en_out_reg, indicating whether the multiplication operation is enabled.
        Input registers, mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg, are updated with the input values when the input enable signal is active.
        Partial product generation is performed using conditional assignments, selecting the appropriate bits from the multiplicand based on the multiplier bits.
        Partial sums are calculated by adding the partial products together and stored in the corresponding registers, sum.
        Final product calculation involves summing up all the partial sums and storing the result in the register, mul_out_reg.
        Output assignment checks if the output enable signal is active, assigning the value in mul_out_reg to mul_out if so, otherwise setting mul_out to 0.
    </implementation>
</module>
